title: Super Senses

title: World's Wildest City: Anchorage
subTitle: Anchorage is home to a huge number of wild porcupines, living in the city's parks, gardens and streets; almost twice the average size, Anchorage's porcupines search for food in the city and find refuge during Alaskan winters.

title: Wild About
subTitle: With only a handful of snakes potentially dangerous to humans and most targeting rodents and pests, it seems their negative reputation today is unearned; a product of their strange limb-less appearance, rather than their nature.

title: Wild About
subTitle: Many of the larger breeds of raptors are now endangered or vulnerable, particularly eagles, vultures and larger owls as they require large, specialized habitats; though they may be soaring high now, the future doesn't bode well for the great raptors.

title: Wild About
subTitle: All great apes are highly endangered partly due to hunting and illegal pet trade, but mainly due to shrinking habitats; sitting in the tree tops and jungles is a reminder of our past and our responsibilities for the future.

subTitle: Alaska stands as Earth's last true wilderness; Its 600 square miles of pristine habitat host diverse wildlife; Caribou herds roam plains, wolves stalk forests, and brown bears rule rivers in this abundant ecosystem.

subTitle: Anchorage faces record insecurity levels in the Arctic; Animals, not humans, commit crimes, vandalism, burglaries, and petty thefts; Wildlife engages in criminal acts, challenging the city's safety norms.
